name: Lemon-Poppy Seed Bread
recipeIngredient: 5 tablespoons unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine the poppy seeds and milk. Let stand 1 hour to macerate and meld flavors.
2. Preheat oven to 325°F.
3. Cream the butter and sugar in the work bowl of an electric mixer.
4. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
5. Combine the flour, baking powder, lemon zest, and salt in a small bowl.
6. Add the dry ingredients to the creamed mixture in three equal portions, alternating with the poppy seed milk. Beat just until smooth.
7. Pour the batter into a greased 9-by-5-inch loaf pan and bake in the center of the preheated oven until golden brown and a cake tester inserted into the center of the loaf comes out clean, about 55 to 65 minutes. Place the loaf on a cooling rack.
8. Meanwhile, to make the lemon syrup, combine the sugar and lemon juice in a small saucepan. Place over low heat just until the sugar dissolves. Set aside.
9. Pierce the hot loaf about a dozen times with a bamboo skewer, toothpick, or metal cake tester. Immediately pour over the hot lemon syrup. Cool 30 minutes before turning out of the pan onto a rack to cool completely. Wrap tightly in plastic wrap and let stand at room temperature overnight before serving.
